executive_summary: Current global systems exhibit localised volatility within the Middle Eastern energy sector alongside elevated narrative anxiety regarding Taiwan. Despite these price fluctuations and media noise, physical indicators and global fuel reserves remain stable and well buffered. The overall systemic risk is contained due to moderate supply chain pressures and an absence of physical escalation in key strategic theatres.
correlations: The acute volatility in Middle East energy is insulated from causing wider systemic harm by the high stability of global fuel reserves.
